text:
Once upon a time in a quiet little town, there lived a curious goose named Gobblefoot. Gobblefoot was known far and wide for her extraordinary curiosity. Every morning, she would squawk down the avenue, her webbed feet clicking against the dew-soaked grass, searching for new wonders.

One crisp autumn morning, Gobblefoot set her sights on Old Mill Pond. She’d heard tales of reflection triangles – little triangles made of ice that seemed to reflect the sky back into itself. It seemed like magic.

Approaching the water's edge, Gobblefoot scanned the ice. There it was, a shimmering triangle, standing alone. Gobblefoot hopped closer, curious. Suddenly, a tiny hole appeared at the center. Excited, she pecked at it. Nothing happened.

Another triangle appeared, similar to the first. Gobblefoot hopped right into it. Suddenly, she was surrounded by even smaller triangles, swirling around her, forming a miniature ice palace
